title: westringia fitzgeraldensis
intro: Westringia fitzgeraldensis is a species of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae, commonly known as a native Australian shrub. This plant has been traditionally used in Australian herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Westringia fitzgeraldensis include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Westringia fitzgeraldensis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a versatile and effective herbal remedy.
